_meta.description: DataStream API в Python, ProcessFunction, state

questions.question: Какие features доступны в PyFlink DataStream API в Flink 2.2, а какие нет?

options.text: PyFlink DataStream имеет 100% parity с Java

**Available**: source/sink builders, transformations (map, filter, flat_map, key_by, process), ProcessFunction/KeyedProcessFunction (since 1.14), side outputs (OutputTag), watermark strategies, state primitives (ValueState, ListState, MapState), timers (event + processing time), connected streams. **NOT available**: CEP API (Java only), DataStream V2 (FLIP-409, Java only), complex window assigners (limited subset, use Table API), full SinkV2 builder ecosystem, some advanced AsyncFunction patterns. Async UDF в 2.2+ closes большинство gaps
PyFlink DataStream deprecated в favor of Table API

questions.explanation: PyFlink DataStream API evolved через releases. By Flink 2.2 covered все main production patterns но still имеет gaps vs Java. **First-class в Python**: Source/sink (KafkaSource, JdbcSink, etc.), basic transformations, KeyedProcessFunction (since 1.14 — biggest add), side outputs through OutputTag, watermark strategies (WatermarkStrategy, TimestampAssigner), state primitives (ValueState/ListState/MapState — registered through RuntimeContext в open()), timers (register_event_time_timer/register_processing_time_timer), connected streams (connect + CoProcessFunction). **Missing/limited**: (1) FlinkCEP — Java only, no Python wrapper. (2) DataStream V2 (FLIP-409) — experimental new API, Java only currently. (3) Custom partitioners — limited. (4) Some Sink V2 builder customizations. (5) Advanced window assigners (Tumbling/Sliding work, custom assigners challenging). Async UDFs в Flink 2.2 (см. урок 08) closes async I/O gap. **Production guidance**: Table API имеет first-class parity с Java (см. урок 04), so for ETL-style use Table API. DataStream Python для cases requiring custom keyed state с complex logic, custom timers, or low-level Process functions.

questions.question: Differences PyFlink DataStream vs Java DataStream API — что характеризует?

options.text: (1) type_info OBLIGATORY — Java's TypeExtractor выводит types из generics, Python нет — нужно explicit `output_type=Types.*()`. (2) `yield` generator pattern вместо `out.collect()` — pythonic, multi-yield supported. (3) Lambdas работают без issues (explicit type info eliminates erasure problems). (4) Python native tuple вместо Flink Tuple2/3 — automatic conversion. (5) Performance gap: process mode ~30-50% baseline, thread mode (PEMJA) ~70-90%, Pandas UDF ~90-95%. (6) Cloudpickle для arbitrary Python objects — fallback Types.PICKLED_BYTE_ARRAY when no explicit type
Python только slower версия Java API
Java API использует POJO, Python нет

questions.explanation: **Diff 1 — type_info obligatory**: Java TypeExtractor uses generic signatures (`MapFunction<A, B>`). Python: typing hints не подходят (PEP 484 hints не enforced runtime). Flink requires explicit `output_type=Types.STRING()` etc. Без — cloudpickle fallback (~10x slower) или error. **Diff 2 — yield generator**: Java `out.collect(value)` imperative. Python `yield value` — generator pattern, more Pythonic. Multi-yield в одном `process_element` supported. Both work, but no batch emit. **Diff 3 — Lambdas work**: Java has erasure issues (см. M11 урок 04). Python lambdas + explicit type info — clean, no problems. Common pattern: `.map(lambda r: r[0], output_type=Types.INT())`. **Diff 4 — Native tuples**: Java uses flink.Tuple2/3 with f0/f1 fields. Python: native `(a, b, c)` tuples. PyFlink automatically converts. TypeInformation: `Types.TUPLE([Types.STRING(), Types.INT()])`. **Diff 5 — Performance**: Multiple modes — process (30-50% baseline, urок 05), thread/PEMJA (70-90%, урок 06), Pandas (90-95%, урок 07), async (depends external, урок 08). Choice depends workload characteristics. **Diff 6 — Cloudpickle fallback**: для arbitrary Python objects (custom classes без explicit type), Flink uses cloudpickle. Includes class bytecode in serialization → ~10x slower, larger wire size. Avoid via explicit Types.* declarations.
questions.studyHint: Повторите урок 03 — секцию 'Differences от Java API — пятёрка важных'

questions.question: Pattern для KeyedProcessFunction в PyFlink с ValueState и timers — какой?

options.text: PyFlink не поддерживает state и timers

Class extends KeyedProcessFunction. open(runtime_context) — register state via ValueStateDescriptor(name, type), self.state = runtime_context.get_state(descriptor). process_element(value, ctx) — access state via self.state.value()/update()/clear(), register timers via ctx.timer_service().register_event_time_timer(ts). on_timer(timestamp, ctx) — callback when timer fires, yield results. Same pattern как Java, но Python yield instead of Collector
Используется DataStream API напрямую без classes

questions.studyHint: Повторите урок 03 — секцию 'ProcessFunction / KeyedProcessFunction в Python'

questions.question: Python ProcessFunction может block watermark progression если process_element медленный — в Java mailbox processor explicitly schedules watermark forwarding, в Python — sequential execution. Watermark stalls приводят к delayed window emissions downstream. Mitigation: Pandas UDFs (vectorized), thread mode (PEMJA), monitor watermark gap metrics.
questions.explanation: Mailbox processor model: Flink JVM has single-threaded mailbox per subtask processing records, watermarks, checkpoint barriers, timers in FIFO order. In Java, Flink can interleave fast operations (forward watermark) с slow ones. В Python — process mode имеет additional IPC layer (см. урок 05), thread mode (PEMJA) has GIL contention. Slow Python UDF (say 100ms per record) blocks entire mailbox процесса — watermark events queue behind. Downstream effects: windowed operators receive watermark late, window emission delayed, processing time лагает за event time. **Symptoms**: `flink_taskmanager_job_task_operator_currentWatermark` metric stuck or progresses much slower than event time. Window emissions сдвигаются. End-to-end latency дoes up. **Mitigation strategies**: (1) **Pandas UDFs (урок 07)**: vectorize Python operations — 100 records processed в batch ~5ms (vs 100×10ms row-wise). Watermark progresses между batches. (2) **Thread mode PEMJA (урок 06)**: eliminates IPC overhead, per-record processing ~5x faster vs process mode. (3) **Async UDFs (урок 08)**: для external I/O specifically — concurrent in-flight requests, no blocking single record. (4) **Reduce parallelism per slot** if GIL contention в thread mode. (5) **Monitor**: `currentWatermark` per operator, `processBundleMs` для Python operators. Alert if watermark gap > expected based on bounded out-of-orderness setting.
